title: eremopyrum bonaepartis var. turkestanicum
intro: Eremopyrum bonaepartis var. turkestanicum, also known as Turkestan wheat grass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Turkestan wheat grass can be consumed as a herbal tea or supplement, with some herbal preparations involving the use of its extracts to create capsules, tinctures, and other products.
